aliases: Steamed bread with kaya and butter

english: Steamed bread with kaya and butter

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general steamed bread entry; no specific kaya-butter steamed bread entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — steamed bread analogue
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated kaya butter steamed bread code. A000E (bread and similar products) used as the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for kaya butter steamed bread as a specific food concept.

main_ingredients: kaya (coconut jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, pandan)

serving.description: 2 pieces of steamed bread with kaya and butter
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving at a Singaporean kopitiam consists of two pieces of steamed white bread (approximately 120 g total) spread with kaya and a slice of cold butter.

nutrient_source.title: Kaya Butter Steamed Bread — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Kaya Butter Steamed Bread (F05020059). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 6.5 g/100 g based on the known fat composition of butter and coconut milk; this specific sub-fraction is not separately reported in the HPB record and is documented as an approximation.

gi_source.title: White bread (steamed) — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to kaya butter steamed bread
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 75 for white steamed bread (mantou-style). Kaya butter steamed bread uses the same refined wheat flour steamed base; the addition of kaya (high sugar) and butter does not meaningfully lower the GI of the bread component. GI 75 (high) is applied as a defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 48.72) / 100 ≈ 36.5, based on 120 g serving containing 40.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (15.9 g/100 g) and elevated sodium (347 mg/100 g) with significant saturated fat from butter and coconut milk kaya
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid kaya butter steamed bread as a regular breakfast item due to its high sugar content (15.9 g/100 g), which independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance, combined with borderline sodium (347 mg/100 g) and saturated fat (~6.5 g/100 g) that contribute to arterial stiffness; if consumed, limit to an occasional small portion and avoid pairing with other high-sodium or high-sugar foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (40.6 g/100 g), high sugar (15.9 g/100 g exceeding 15 g/100 g threshold), low dietary fibre (0.9 g/100 g), and high GI (75)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kaya butter steamed bread is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high GI of 75, sugar content of 15.9 g/100 g, and 40.6 g/100 g of refined carbohydrate with only 0.9 g/100 g dietary fibre, producing a rapid and significant postprandial glucose spike; if consumed at all, restrict to a very small portion paired with a protein source and monitor blood glucose response closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 6.5 g/100 g from butter and coconut milk, trans fat 0.1 g/100 g, high sugar 15.9 g/100 g, and sodium 347 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kaya butter steamed bread poses a high cardiovascular concern due to its saturated fat content (~6.5 g/100 g from butter and coconut milk kaya), trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), high sugar (15.9 g/100 g), and borderline sodium (347 mg/100 g), all of which contribute to dyslipidaemia, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this food or consume it only very occasionally in small portions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~6.5 g/100 g (above 5 g/100 g RA threshold), sugar 15.9 g/100 g (above 15 g/100 g RA threshold), and trace trans fat 0.1 g/100 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid kaya butter steamed bread due to its pro-inflammatory saturated fat (~6.5 g/100 g from butter and coconut milk), high sugar (15.9 g/100 g amplifying inflammatory cytokine production), and trace trans fat (0.1 g/100 g), particularly during active flares.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kaya butter steamed bread is a classic Singaporean kopitiam breakfast dish consisting of soft, pillowy steamed white bread rolls served with generous portions of kaya — a fragrant Southeast Asian coconut jam made from coconut milk, eggs, sugar, and pandan leaves — and cold slices of salted butter. The bread itself is made from refined wheat flour and steamed rather than baked, giving it a distinctively soft, moist, and slightly chewy texture with a pale white exterior. The combination of sweet, aromatic kaya and rich, salty butter creates a beloved sweet-savoury flavour contrast that is deeply embedded in Singaporean food culture. Nutritionally, kaya butter steamed bread is energy-dense at 290 kcal per 100 g, with 40.6 g of refined carbohydrate, 15.9 g of sugar, 11.51 g of fat (approximately 6.5 g saturated from butter and coconut milk), and only 0.9 g of dietary fibre. Sodium is 347 mg/100 g. The glycemic index is high (approximately 75), making it a rapid glucose-spiking food. It is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis when consumed regularly. It is commonly eaten at breakfast in kopitiams (traditional Singaporean coffee shops) and is typically paired with a soft-boiled egg and kopi (local coffee). The dish is also known as kaya butter mantou in Chinese-speaking contexts.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05020059) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from known ingredient composition (butter and coconut milk kaya) and documented as an approximation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white steamed bread, which is the same base product. All mandatory multilingual names are present for a Singaporean dish.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid sub-fraction data for kaya butter steamed bread are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F05020059) as of 2026. potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo record for this dish and are not available from an approved secondary source for this specific preparation. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.1 g/100 g based on the known presence of trace trans fats in butter; this is an ingredient-level estimate and not directly measured in the HPB record. saturated_fat_g of 6.5 g/100 g is estimated from the known fat composition of butter (~50% saturated) and coconut milk (~85% saturated fat of fat fraction); the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat for this dish. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white steamed bread (mantou-style); no direct GI measurement for kaya butter steamed bread was found in the approved GI database. Glycemic load per serving calculated at 120 g serving size: GL = (75 × 48.72) / 100 ≈ 36.5.
